topic: Transitioning from procedural to Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) in p5.js, including advanced styling with the HTML5 Canvas API and clipping masks.

concepts.name: Classes and Instances
concepts.explanation: A class is a 'template' or blueprint that defines properties and behaviors, while an instance is the actual object created from that template using the 'new' keyword.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to manage hundreds of unique objects (like disks) without writing separate code for every single one.

concepts.name: Constructor
concepts.explanation: A special function within a class that runs once when an object is created to set its initial 'state' (like position, speed, and size) using the 'this' keyword.
concepts.why_it_matters: It ensures every new object has its own unique data (e.g., different random speeds) while following the same blueprint.

concepts.name: Drawing Context
concepts.explanation: The underlying Web API (CanvasRenderingContext2D) that p5.js wraps. You access it via `drawingContext` to use advanced features like radial gradients that aren't in standard p5.js functions.
concepts.why_it_matters: It unlocks professional-grade visual effects like complex gradients and shadows.

concepts.name: Radial Gradients
concepts.explanation: A color transition that radiates from a center point, created using `createRadialGradient` with six parameters (start x/y, start radius, end x/y, and end radius) and `addColorStop` for color stops.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows for much more sophisticated and organic-looking color transitions than simple linear fills.

concepts.name: Clipping and Masking
concepts.explanation: Using `beginClip()` and `endClip()` to define a boundary. Anything drawn between these two commands is restricted to the shape of that boundary, acting as a mask.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to contain complex animations or patterns within specific geometric shapes.

concepts.name: Push and Pop (Isolation)
concepts.explanation: Using `push()` and `pop()` to isolate transformations like `translate` or `rotate`, ensuring that changes made to the coordinate system only affect the current object.
concepts.why_it_matters: Without this, a transformation applied to one object would 'leak' and affect every subsequent object drawn.

code_examples.demonstrates: Basic procedural movement and collision detection (bouncing) for a single circle.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to reset the position or reverse the speed when hitting a boundary.

code_examples.demonstrates: Refactoring procedural movement into a Class structure with encapsulated state and display logic.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to use `this.` when referencing class properties inside methods.

code_examples.demonstrates: Using the drawingContext to create a radial gradient and applying it via color stops.
code_examples.common_issue: Trying to use `fill()` directly on the gradient object instead of using `drawingContext.fillStyle`.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The object doesn't move or the code errors out because 'x' is undefined.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Forgetting to use the `this.` keyword when referencing class properties inside a method.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Objects fall off the bottom of the screen and never come back.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Checking `this.y > height` where `height` is a property of the object rather than the canvas.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Ensure you are checking against the global `height` variable, not a non-existent property on the object.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Error: [object Arguments] is not a valid color representation.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Attempting to use `fill()` (a p5 function) on a gradient object instead of using the underlying `drawingContext.fillStyle` property.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use `drawingContext.fillStyle = this.fill;` to apply the gradient.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: Shadows are not appearing or are behaving strangely.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Trying to attach shadow properties (like `shadowBlur`) directly to the gradient object instead of the drawing context.

key_moments.description: The instructor begins the transition from simple procedural code to a Class structure, showing how variables become 'state'.
The instructor explains that p5.js is essentially a 'wrapper' around the more powerful HTML5 Canvas API (drawingContext).
The instructor demonstrates that shadows are properties, not methods, and warns about the performance cost of using them.
Introduction of clipping masks to contain visual elements within specific shapes.

prerequisites: Basic understanding of the draw loop
student_questions: Why are all my circles in one spot?
How do I make hundreds of shapes without writing a hundred lines of code?
What is the difference between a class and an object?
How do I make a gradient that isn't just a simple color change?
Why is my code slow when I add shadows?
How do I make a shape act like a mask so things only show up inside it?
What is 'this' in JavaScript and why do I need it for my class?
How do I use CSS styles in p5.js?
Why is my gradient not showing up even though I defined the colors?
How do I make an object rotate around its own center?
